source.title: Explosion, Molten Salt Eruption, and Fatal Injury at TS USA Liquid Nitriding Facility

context.operator: Techniques Surfaces USA (TS USA), subsidiary of HEF Groupe

event.top_event: Molten salt eruption and steam explosion
actions_taken: Facility shut down for approximately eight months
Protective enclosure installed at oxidizer bath after incident
Employee treated on-site by Chattanooga Fire Department
Multiple fires extinguished by TS USA employees and CFD personnel
event.summary: On May 30, 2024, at 8:58 a.m., an explosion occurred at the TS USA Chattanooga liquid nitriding facility while processing five rollers with internal cavities. The rollers had been submerged in molten salt baths during the nitriding process. Due to small drain holes (0.2 inches diameter), molten salt was unable to fully drain from one roller and solidified into a plug. During a subsequent hot water rinse, water became trapped in the cavity behind the salt plug. When the rollers were reintroduced into the 800°F oxidizer bath to clear the obstruction, the trapped water boiled and rapidly expanded as steam, creating overpressure inside the roller cavity. The press fit end of the roller dislodged, ejecting water into the molten salt and causing a steam explosion. The explosion erupted approximately 4,500 pounds of molten sodium hydroxide and sodium nitrate from the oxidizer bath, engulfing a line operator and causing second- and third-degree chemical and thermal burns over 95 percent of his body. The operator died later that day at the hospital. Three other employees suffered minor burns. The incident caused approximately $1.3 million in property damage and multiple fires throughout the facility.
recommendations: Implement physical protective barriers around molten salt baths to isolate employees from hazardous releases
Develop comprehensive safety management system including hazard analysis program, written operating procedures, training program, and incident investigation program
Establish position with specific expertise in safety management systems at each TS USA facility
Include physical protective barriers as standard design for liquid nitriding processes at HEF Groupe
Develop knowledge management program for sharing information from audits, hazard analyses, and incident investigations throughout HEF Groupe
Develop and implement comprehensive corporate governance program with regular audits of subordinate facilities

hazards.name: Water or moisture in process equipment
Cavities and internal voids in metal parts
Sodium hydroxide and sodium nitrate (oxidizing salts)

threats.name: Accumulation of molten salt in roller cavity
Solidification of salt plug blocking drain hole
Water retention in roller cavity due to blocked drain
Reintroduction of water-containing roller into hot salt bath
Boiling and vaporization of trapped water
Overpressure buildup in sealed roller cavity
Failure of roller press fit end due to overpressure
Contact of water with molten salt causing steam explosion

consequences.name: Molten salt eruption from oxidizer bath
Exposure of personnel to molten salt
Severe thermal and chemical burns to line operator
Minor burns to three other employees

controls.name: Hazard analysis program for nitriding process
controls.barrier_role: Identify accumulation hazards and sealed cavity risks before processing

supporting_text: TS USA lacked hazard analyses on processing new parts, on the liquid nitriding line, and for reprocessing parts

Due to the lack of these programs, on the day of the incident, TS USA employees were unprotected from and unaware of the hazards presented by the nitriding line, entrapment of materials in the parts, and reprocessing the rollers

controls.name: Written operating procedures for nitriding process
controls.barrier_role: Establish safe work practices and prevent unsafe reprocessing decisions

supporting_text: TS USA lacked procedures for reprocessing parts in the oxidizer bath
The plant manager contacted the process engineer for guidance on how to clear the obstruction. The process engineer recommended reintroducing the rollers directly into the oxidizing bath

controls.name: Training program on nitriding process hazards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel understand accumulation hazards and proper handling procedures
controls.lod_basis: Build competence to recognize and avoid hazards

supporting_text: TS USA did not have training on the hazards present in the nitriding line
TS USA employees were unprotected from and unaware of the hazards presented by the nitriding line, entrapment of materials in the parts, and reprocessing the rollers

controls.name: Part design review and acceptance criteria
controls.barrier_role: Reject parts with unsuitable cavity designs before processing
controls.lod_basis: Prevent unsuitable parts from entering the process

supporting_text: Before accepting the rollers for processing in the nitriding line, the process engineer had asked the customer whether the main body of the roller had a sealed cavity

The customer confirmed that the rollers had cavities and through-holes on each end, which were plugged with set screws. At TS USA's request, the set screws were removed by the customer from the through-holes to open the sealed cavity

With the drain holes opened, TS USA did not consider the rollers to be sealed and, therefore, safe to process in the liquid nitriding line. However, due to the configuration of the cavity and the small size of the drain hole, the rollers still presented an accumulation hazard. This hazard was not recognized by TS USA's management systems and reviewers
The CSB concludes that the rollers were not suitable for processing through the liquid nitriding process. The drain holes were not large enough to ensure the rollers fully drained the molten salts

controls.name: Verification that parts are fully drained before cooling
controls.barrier_role: Detect and prevent water/salt retention in cavities
controls.lod_basis: Detect accumulation before it becomes hazardous

supporting_text: At 7:09 a.m., on May 30, 2024, a TS USA line operator removed the rollers from the rinse bath containing 160–180℉ water. The rollers were suspended over the rinse tank to allow water to drain from the parts and then moved to the end of the production line to cool down. However, the water was unable to drain out of the one roller with the solidified salt plug blocking its drain hole

Although some of the water drained from the roller, it stopped draining before the cavity was empty. Due to the lack of any additional water draining from the parts, the plant manager believed that the part only contained solidified oxidizer salt and no water. No further actions, such as turning the rollers over, were taken to verify that there were no other materials in the roller

controls.name: Protective barrier around oxidizer bath
controls.barrier_role: Isolate personnel from molten salt eruption
controls.lod_basis: Prevent personnel exposure to released molten salt

supporting_text: The line operator was standing behind the protective shielding, less than three feet from the opening of the oxidizer bath, while reintroducing the rollers to the bath
Implement physical, protective barriers around the molten salt baths that isolate employees from hazardous releases at all locations that perform liquid nitriding

controls.name: Incident investigation and knowledge sharing program
controls.barrier_role: Learn from past incidents and prevent recurrence
controls.lod_basis: Prevent similar incidents through organizational learning

supporting_text: Before the 2024 fatal eruption in Chattanooga, HEF Groupe had at least three similar incidents involving the processing of parts with accumulation hazards or sealed cavities
TS USA and HEF Groupe did not ensure that these safety incidents were investigated or that lessons learned from events were shared and incorporated across the organization to prevent future incidents
2018 Event in Mexico
2020 Event in France
2023 Event in Chattanooga

controls.name: Corporate governance and knowledge management
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safety information and hazard analyses are communicated to all facilities

supporting_text: HEF Groupe developed hazard analyses for the nitriding process. However, the details of these analyses and the safeguards were not communicated to HEF Groupe's subsidiary companies, including TS USA

HEF Groupe did not ensure that its subsidiaries were following the company's guidance, such as the risk assessments and safety alert letters, developed at the corporate level to ensure the safety of the operations
HEF Groupe did not ensure that the safety knowledge maintained at the corporate level was communicated to its subsidiary companies to promote the safe operation of the liquid nitriding line

controls.name: Drain hole design standards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure adequate drainage of molten salt from part cavities
controls.lod_basis: Prevent salt accumulation and plug formation

supporting_text: The drain holes were very small—0.03 square inches, or just 0.25 percent of the cross-sectional area of the cavities, which resulted in slow drain times
As a result, the molten salt was unable to fully drain out of one of the rollers before it cooled and solidified, likely as a thick disk or plug
The CSB concludes that the rollers were not suitable for processing through the liquid nitriding process. The drain holes were not large enough to ensure the rollers fully drained the molten salts

controls.name: Detection of water retention in parts
controls.barrier_role: Identify water-containing parts before reintroduction to hot salt bath

supporting_text: After the rollers had cooled for over an hour, the plant manager and supervisor observed that at least one roller was too hot to continuously touch

Additionally, water was still slowly draining from the one roller that was too hot to touch. The drain hole appeared to be clogged, which the plant manager and supervisor believed to be due to oxidizer salt

Although some of the water drained from the roller, it stopped draining before the cavity was empty. Due to the lack of any additional water draining from the parts, the plant manager believed that the part only contained solidified oxidizer salt and no water. No further actions, such as turning the rollers over, were taken to verify that there were no other materials in the roller

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.

pif_evidence.people_competence: TS USA employees were unaware of accumulation hazards and did not recognize the danger of water retention in the roller cavity. The approximate average tenure of employees was 2.5 years with no employee having more than five years of experience.
pif_evidence.people_communication: The plant manager contacted the process engineer for guidance, but the process engineer recommended reintroducing the rollers into the oxidizing bath without adequate hazard analysis or consideration of water retention risks.
pif_evidence.people_situational_awareness: The plant manager and supervisor believed the roller contained only solidified salt and no water, despite evidence of water draining from the roller and the roller being too hot to touch.
pif_evidence.work_procedures: TS USA lacked written operating procedures for the nitriding process and specifically lacked procedures for reprocessing parts in the oxidizer bath.
pif_evidence.work_tools_equipment: The plant manager and supervisor attempted to clear the obstruction using a piece of wire but were unsuccessful. No other tools or methods were employed to verify the contents of the roller cavity.
pif_evidence.organisation_safety_culture: TS USA did not have an adequate process safety management system in place at its Chattanooga facility. The company lacked hazard analyses, training programs, and incident investigation procedures.
pif_evidence.organisation_management_of_change: TS USA accepted new parts (rollers with cavities) without conducting adequate hazard analysis or assessing the suitability of the parts for the nitriding process.
pif_evidence.organisation_supervision: The plant manager made the decision to reintroduce the rollers into the oxidizing bath based on guidance from a remote process engineer without adequate hazard analysis or verification of the roller contents.
pif_evidence.organisation_training: TS USA did not have training on the hazards present in the nitriding line. Employees were unaware of accumulation hazards and the dangers of water in molten salt baths.
